self_improvement.claim: Directive Workspace can keep the canonical truth anchor useful by resolving a known historical Runtime follow-up directly instead of crashing reports.
self_improvement.mechanism: This DEEP bounded result adds a read-only legacy Runtime follow-up focus path to the shared state resolver.
self_improvement.baseline_observation: Before this DEEP case, the canonical report crashed on the deferred CLI-anything Runtime follow-up even though the host already treated it as readable legacy history.
self_improvement.expected_effect: Future report and composition work can inspect that deferred Runtime artifact directly without reconstructing its state by hand.

decision.rationale: The shared truth anchor should resolve known historical Runtime follow-ups as read-only artifacts instead of crashing.
decision.runtime_threshold_check: yes - the mechanism is still valuable without a runtime surface, so Architecture should retain product-owned value
